principles: GameSemanticSource and the semantic layout dictionary own spatial intent; neither owns resolved coordinates.
Accepted AssetWorld owns resource acceptance. Spatial planning consumes accepted resources without judging, replacing, or regenerating them.

SpatialPlanner receives one hash-bound semantic view, component-expansion facts, ordered accepted-image references, and a GDevelop-evidence-derived planning space, then emits a direct GDJS-coordinate candidate.
Spatial Runtime validates candidates against hash-bound intent, native geometry, scene-canvas facts, and GDJS coordinate facts. It does not generate a first layout.
Accepted spatial-layout-resolution is the only canonical spatial result. GDJS projection and preview are derived evidence only.
The GDJS scene canvas is a project fact used for first assembly. It is neither a device profile nor another layout source.
Layout dictionary records express legal regions, layer and z-order bounds, overlap policy, and anchor preferences; they never resolve scene coordinates for SpatialPlanner.
Planner trace artifacts preserve every raw DSL response and external-round fact flow as diagnostic evidence; they never become spatial truth.

truths.componentFacts: semantic-component-expansion derived from the same GameSemanticSource
truths.gdjsSceneCanvas: asset-bound GDJS project properties, selected scene, layers, and camera facts
truths.gdjsCoordinateModel: ai/gdevelop-truth/spatial-coordinate-truth.json generated from the pinned GDevelop runtime revision
truths.planningSpace: deterministic projection of pinned GDevelop coordinate truth, the selected GDJS scene canvas, and the semantic layout dictionary
truths.plannerCandidate: spatial-layout-candidate proposal bound to one spatial-assembly-input

render-geometry.drawableBoundsSemantic: native drawable boundary relative to the object origin
render-geometry.nativeSizeSemantic: accepted asset native pixel size before SpatialPlanner chooses display size
render-geometry.objectOriginSemantic: asset-local point represented by the GDevelop object position

candidateProgram.firstCandidate: one complete PLACE program for every scene-instance subject
candidateProgram.acceptance: ACCEPT is a standalone later-round command for an already projected and previewed candidate

documents.spatial-assembly-request: semantic-stage request with dictionary intent, reservation, and required facts only
documents.spatial-geometry-fact-set: accepted native drawable and GDevelop coordinate-model facts for one source hash
documents.spatial-layout-intent-snapshot: immutable, hash-bound planner view derived from exactly one semantic layout plan
documents.gdevelop-spatial-coordinate-truth: generated fixed-version evidence for GDJS coordinates, initial camera mapping, layer stacking, and z-order scope
documents.spatial-planning-space: derived coordinate frame, layer stack, resolved camera view, and dictionary legal pixel regions for one selected scene
documents.spatial-assembly-input: fully hash-bound input containing scene-canvas facts, planning space, and planner subjects
documents.spatial-planner-context: hash-bound projection of source semantics, component expansion, ordered accepted-image references, and the derived GDJS planning space
documents.gdjs-spatial-projection: GDevelop instance projection derived from either a validated candidate for preview evidence or the accepted resolution for final assembly
documents.gdjs-spatial-preview: rendered evidence derived only from gdjs-spatial-projection
documents.spatial-layout-resolution: the sole accepted canonical spatial result
documents.spatial-planner-model-output: immediately persisted raw DSL plus exact prompt and ordered image inputs for one external round
documents.spatial-planner-round-trace: persisted parse, validation, projection, preview, execution, and feedback evidence for one external round
documents.spatial-planner-run-trace: persisted run-level index of every round and trace entry

requires: A canonical producer must bind every render-geometry fact to its accepted AssetWorld slot or FrameSet frame, or to component-expansion evidence.
The producer must record source hash, artifact hash, producer revision, native drawable bounds, native size, and object origin as one auditable fact set.
The generated GDevelop spatial coordinate truth must bind the selected GDevelop commit and every source file used for coordinate, camera, layer, and z-order semantics.
Every gdjs-coordinate-contract fact and planning space must bind that exact generated truth.
Runtime must reject ACCEPT in the same round as PLACE.
Runtime must bind acceptance to the exact prior GDJS projection and preview evidence.
Spatial Assembly Input must preserve complete GDJS camera size and viewport facts.
Multi-camera or custom camera framing must fail before planning until its scene-coordinate mapping is implemented.
Final projection must derive from spatial-layout-resolution only after acceptance.
Candidate projection, preview, planner traces, and planner candidates are evidence only and cannot be read as final spatial truth.
